title: Pi remote: setup wizard re-appears after reboot, settings not persisted
description: A pi_remote device that was successfully configured (server URL + API key entered) falls back to the setup wizard after a reboot. is_configured() returns False because remote_server_url and/or upstream_api_key are not present in settings.json. Root causes to investigate: (1) settings.save() not called after the pi_remote wizard completes; (2) DATA_DIR environment variable not set in the systemd unit, so settings.json is written to a temp path; (3) the satellite config pull overwrites settings.json before is_configured() can read the pi_remote fields. Fix: ensure wizard save includes deployment_mode + remote_server_url + upstream_api_key, and that DATA_DIR is correctly set in the venv service unit.
close_reason: Completed the fix by allowing empty/blank environment variables to be overridden by settings.json during the overlay load.

title: First-run pi_hosted: kiosk service, Stream Deck service, and other installs fail on fresh image
description: On a fresh pi_hosted image first boot, multiple services fail to install: kiosk (Chromium autostart) fails, Stream Deck service pip install fails, and possibly others. Root causes to investigate: (1) Python venv not yet created when service installer runs; (2) apt packages not yet installed (chromium-browser, python3-dev, libhidapi-libusb0); (3) systemd unit files reference paths that do not exist yet; (4) install order in firstboot.sh may be wrong. Fix: audit firstboot.sh install order, add pre-flight dependency checks, and capture install failures to a log file readable from the setup UI.
close_reason: Completed the fix by ensuring apt-get update is run before targeted installs, adding retry loops, checking python3-venv health, and fixing test compatibility.

title: WiFi fallback AP mode: if Pi cannot connect to WiFi, create hotspot and redirect to captive portal WiFi setup
description: On first boot (or when saved WiFi credentials fail) the Pi should fall into a fallback hotspot mode instead of hanging. Requirements: (1) firstboot.sh detects WiFi connect failure after N seconds; (2) if failed, configure hostapd + dnsmasq to create an AP with SSID FoodAssistant-Setup and a fixed password; (3) all DNS from connected clients resolves to the Pi so any URL opens the captive portal; (4) captive portal page shows WiFi SSID scanner and credential entry form; (5) on submit, write wpa_supplicant config and restart networking; (6) on success, disable AP mode and reboot into normal mode. Captive portal should work on iOS, Android, and desktop browsers.

title: Decide: merge arch/modular into main
description: The arch/modular branch is substantially complete. Remaining gaps: periodic satellite sync (lvc), sync status UI (yz6), pi_remote OTA updates (2sz), and satellite integration tests (yxi). Decision needed: (1) merge now with gaps as follow-up beads on main, or (2) complete all gaps on the branch first. Recommend: merge after yxi (tests) lands to ensure CI passes; land lvc/yz6/2sz post-merge as they are additive features.

title: Decide: testing/CI investment level
description: Repo has no automated tests or CI. Recommend: small pytest suite for pure logic (recipe matching, barcode heuristics, defaults rules) + GitHub Actions lint+test on push. Decide scope before Phase 2.

title: Decide: backup strategy for data volumes
description: service/data (SQLite + settings.json incl. secrets), grocy/config, mealie/data are bind mounts with no backup. Decide: simple cron tar, restic, or host-level snapshots.

title: Decide: approve/reorder proposed feature phases 3-4
description: Review the Phase 3 (Mealie deepening) and Phase 4 (polish) beads and approve, drop, or reprioritize. Top suggestions: missing-ingredients->shopping-list button; Cooked-it inventory consumption; HA meal plan sensors.

title: Decide: auth posture (Pangolin vs built-in) + Mealie admin creds
description: Currently Pangolin fronts public access and built-in AUTH_PASSWORD/API key are optional. Decide the standard: rely on proxy auth, built-in auth, or both. Also change Mealie default changeme@example.com login.

title: Review: delete stale GitHub branch claude/vibrant-goldberg-2z2hy
description: History was rewritten so all commits on main are authored by Dan. The old branch still holds pre-rewrite commits; deletion was blocked from the cloud session ΓÇö delete it in the GitHub UI (Branches page).

title: Review: field-test the new Mealie integration
description: docker compose --profile with-mealie up -d; change default admin creds; create API token (User Profile -> API Tokens); configure in /setup; verify Meal Plan page, Shopping page, and What-can-I-cook suggestions against real inventory.

title: Review: rotate exposed FoodAssistant API key
description: The X-API-Key starting 081acd96 appeared in chat/shell history during HA debugging. Generate a new key (openssl rand -hex 24), update /setup and homeassistant/configuration.yaml.

title: Phase 0: Discussion ΓÇö review with Dan before continuing
description: Items Dan should review/decide before further feature work. Nothing downstream starts until the relevant item here is closed.

title: Mealie starts but is not accessible: port/network issue on pi_hosted
description: After docker compose up Mealie reports started but the UI at http://<hostname>:3000 is not reachable. Possible causes: (1) Mealie container is binding to 127.0.0.1 instead of 0.0.0.0 — check docker-compose port mapping is \3000:9000\ not \127.0.0.1:3000:9000\; (2) ufw or iptables firewall blocking port 3000; (3) Mealie taking longer than expected to initialize — the health check passes the container started check but the app is not yet serving; (4) docker network bridge not routing correctly on Pi OS. Add a post-start health check to the setup wizard that polls http://localhost:3000/api/app/about with retries and shows a clear error if Mealie never becomes reachable.

title: Pi remote touchscreen not working: Waveshare HDMI display Pi Hat
description: The Waveshare HDMI touchscreen Pi Hat is not recognized as a touch input device on pi_remote. The display works (HDMI output fine) but touch events are not registered. Waveshare displays typically need: (1) a custom dtoverlay in /boot/config.txt (e.g. dtoverlay=waveshare-hdmi-touch); (2) libinput or evdev rules for the USB HID touch controller; (3) Chromium launched with --touch-events=enabled and possibly --use-gl=egl. Add Waveshare touchscreen as a setup wizard option in the display configuration pane. firstboot.sh should apply the correct dtoverlay and udev rules when this display type is selected.

title: Setup wizard: live install progress console window for services (Mealie, Stream Deck, kiosk etc)
description: When pi_hosted setup triggers a service install (Mealie via Docker, Stream Deck service, kiosk/Chromium service) the UI shows no feedback — the user sees a spinner and has no idea if installation is succeeding or stalled. Implement a server-sent events (SSE) endpoint that streams stdout/stderr from the install subprocess in real-time. The setup page should open a scrollable terminal-style console pane that auto-scrolls to the bottom and shows exit status (success/fail) when the process ends. Attach to: Mealie docker pull/up, pip install for streamdeck, kiosk service setup.

title: Mealie URL: replace local IP with mDNS hostname to survive IP changes
description: Mealie is configured with the Pi LAN IP (e.g. http://192.168.1.x:3000) both internally and in public links. This breaks whenever the Pi gets a new DHCP address. Replace the default mealie_base_url and mealie_public_url with the mDNS hostname: http://<hostname>.local:3000. In pi_hosted mode, firstboot.sh should write the mDNS hostname into settings.json defaults so the user never sees a bare IP. Same fix applies to Grocy.
close_reason: Pre-populated Mealie public URL with mDNS local hostname in seed_app_settings on first boot, and suggested it by default in the setup wizard on Pi.

title: Grocy URL: replace localhost/127.0.0.1 with device hostname in setup defaults and links
description: Grocy is configured with http://127.0.0.1:9383 (docker internal) for API calls, but the setup UI shows this address as the browser-facing link. Clicking it fails because 127.0.0.1 from the users browser points to their laptop, not the Pi. Fix: (1) grocy_public_url should default to http://<hostname>.local:9383 (or device LAN IP) not 127.0.0.1; (2) the test-connection button returns 401 on 127.0.0.1:9383 because the Grocy API key is required — ensure test sends the key; (3) document that grocy_base_url stays as the docker-internal address for API calls but grocy_public_url must be the LAN address for browser links. Using a static IP is also fragile — recommend mDNS hostname (.local) as the default.
close_reason: Pre-populated Grocy public URL with mDNS local hostname in seed_app_settings on first boot, suggested it in the wizard on Pi, and verified API key testing works.

title: docker-compose.remote.yml: port 80 conflicts with venv path
description: docker-compose.remote.yml maps 80:9284, but firstboot.sh for pi_remote also binds the venv service directly on port 80 (via iptables PREROUTING). If both are running simultaneously they conflict. Document clearly that docker-compose.remote.yml is an ALTERNATIVE to the venv path, not an addition. Add a guard in firstboot.sh or a README note: if DEPLOYMENT_MODE=pi_remote and Docker is present, decide which path to use. Consider disabling the venv service if the Docker compose is running.

title: Tests: satellite integration tests (happy path + unreachable server)
description: tests/test_satellite.py covers unit-level _apply_config and _apply_defaults. Missing: integration tests for sync_from_upstream() against a mock HTTP server. Need: (1) happy path: mock returns valid config payload, assert fields applied and providers invalidated; (2) unreachable server: assert returns ok=False, existing config untouched; (3) bad API key: assert 401 handled gracefully; (4) partial payload (missing expiry_defaults): assert config applied, defaults skipped.

title: pi_remote venv: OTA update mechanism
description: Pi Remote devices run the FoodAssistant service in a Python venv (no Docker). There is currently no way to update the software on these devices — no image pull, no git pull hook. Options: (a) simple cron git pull + pip install + service restart; (b) systemd path unit watching a version file; (c) in-app update button that calls a host bridge script. Pick one and implement. Install script should pre-wire the chosen mechanism at firstboot.

title: Satellite setup UI: sync status panel and manual retry
description: The pi_remote setup pane shows read-only mirrored settings but gives no feedback about sync health. Add a Sync Status panel showing: last sync timestamp, which fields were applied, and whether the last pull succeeded. Include a Sync Now button that hits /setup/satellite/sync and shows the result inline. This is critical UX for debugging a satellite that cannot reach its server.

title: Satellite: periodic config sync (startup + cron)
description: Currently sync_from_upstream() runs once at app startup (lifespan). A satellite that runs for days will drift from the main server as keys/settings change. Add a background periodic pull: recommend APScheduler or asyncio background task every N minutes (configurable, default 15min). Startup pull should be best-effort and non-blocking; periodic pull should log warnings but never crash the app.

title: arch/modular: three-mode deployment architecture
description: Tracks all work to land the modular deployment architecture from the arch/modular branch into main. The branch introduces three first-class deployment modes (server, pi_hosted, pi_remote) with feature flags, a satellite config-pull protocol, and a no-Docker pi_remote venv path. This epic covers the remaining gaps before the branch is ready to merge.

title: API key security best-practices pass
description: Audit and fix API key handling end to end: (1) never render saved secrets into setup HTML (mask, write-only fields that keep existing value when blank); (2) settings.json file permissions 0600; (3) secrets redacted from logs and error messages; (4) constant-time comparison for X-API-Key (auth_password already uses secrets.compare_digest); (5) document rotation procedure; (6) consider encrypting settings.json secrets at rest with SECRET_KEY. Overlaps 9px.4 (setup HTML echo) ΓÇö implement together.
close_reason: 0600 settings.json, compare_digest for X-API-Key, error redaction, saved-key fallback for tests, rotation notes in Security pane

title: External recipe API options: TheMealDB free/premium, Spoonacular
description: Settings section for external recipe sources: TheMealDB public API (current default, key 1), TheMealDB premium (paid supporter key, more recipes + no rate limits), or Spoonacular (user API key, much larger catalog with findByIngredients endpoint). Provider-style abstraction in services/recipes_external.py: common interface find_recipes_for_ingredients + get_external_recipe; per-source client; selection + keys in settings with test buttons. Spoonacular budget note: free tier is 150 points/day ΓÇö cache aggressively.
close_reason: recipe_source picker (themealdb/spoonacular/off), TheMealDB key support, Spoonacular client with caching, /setup/test/recipes

title: Expand settings: staples list and other tunables
description: Surface hardcoded tunables in the settings UI. Known candidates: pantry staples list (currently _STAPLE_TOKENS in services/mealie.py), perishable threshold days (14), expiring-soon threshold (5), suggestion counts per tier, barcode enrichment on/off per scan source, HA-facing options. Staples editor: tag-style input, persisted to settings.json, classifier reads from settings with current list as default.
close_reason: Staples editor, perishable_days, expiring_soon_days, suggest_per_tier in Settings; classifier reads from settings

title: Redo settings menu with side navigation
description: Replace the single long /setup page with a side-menu layout (Bootstrap nav-pills column + content pane): sections for Inventory (Grocy), AI Providers, Barcode/Enrichment, Recipes (Mealie + external APIs), Interface (tabs/nav), Security (auth/API keys). Keep per-section test buttons and one global save. Mobile: side menu collapses to a top pill row.
close_reason: Side-menu settings layout: Inventory/AI Providers/Recipes/Interface/Security panes, per-section tests, global save

title: User-configurable navigation: show/hide and re-arrange tabs
description: Let users choose which nav tabs are visible and their order. Store as a saveable setting (e.g. nav_tabs list in settings.json). UI: drag-to-reorder list with visibility toggles in the settings page. base.html renders the nav from the configured list instead of hardcoding.
close_reason: nav_order/nav_hidden settings + registry-rendered navbar + show/hide/reorder editor in Settings Interface pane

title: Auto-hide tabs for unconfigured services
description: Tabs whose backing service is not configured should disappear automatically: no Recipes/Meal Plan/Shopping/Cook tabs without Mealie; no vision-dependent UI without a working provider. Auto-hide combines with manual show/hide from the nav-config feature (hidden-by-config wins over configured service). Currently unconfigured Mealie pages show an info alert ΓÇö replace with hidden tab + setup hint elsewhere.
close_reason: Tabs with requires=mealie auto-hide when Mealie unconfigured; manual hide combines; pages reachable by URL

title: Make What-can-I-cook its own tab
description: Move the tiered recipe suggestions (ready/staples/shopping) out of the Meal Plan sidebar into a dedicated /ui/cook page with its own nav tab. More room means: full-width tier sections, recipe images (TheMealDB provides thumbnails), expandable ingredient lists, and the add-to-mealplan action directly from a suggestion. Meal Plan page keeps a compact link/teaser to the new tab.
close_reason: Cook tab live at /ui/cook with images, collapsible ingredients, plan/save/save+list actions

title: UI & Settings overhaul
description: Top-priority batch from Dan: dedicated cook tab, configurable navigation, settings redesign with side menu and expanded options, external recipe API choices.
close_reason: All six children implemented and closed.

title: Tiered recipe recommendations + external recipe DB + shopping list in Lovelace
description: Three recommendation tiers: (1) cookable with stock only, (2) stock + common pantry staples (eggs, butter, flour...), (3) uses perishable stock but needs shopping ΓÇö with add-missing-to-list button. Pull candidate recipes from TheMealDB (free API) in addition to Mealie; selecting an external recipe saves it into Mealie. Add shopping list REST sensor + Lovelace card.
notes: TheMealDB: filter.php?i=<ingredient> then lookup.php?i=<id>, test key '1' is free. Tier definitions: water ignored everywhere; staples list in services; perishable = refrigerated bucket or expiring <=14d.
close_reason: Implemented: 3-tier classifier (ready/staples/shopping) in services/mealie.py, TheMealDB client in services/recipes_external.py with import-to-Mealie, Save+list combo for external shopping-tier recipes, HA shopping summary endpoint + sensor + Lovelace tile and card.

title: Split Mealie internal vs public URL
description: Mealie authenticates only on the LAN (http://192.168.1.170:3000) but the public URL is https://mealie.korolev.link/. A single mealie_base_url is used for both API calls and browser links ΓÇö links in the UI point to the internal address which is unreachable externally. Add a second setting mealie_public_url used only for href generation in templates; all httpx API calls keep using base URL. Setup wizard exposes both fields. Fallback: if public URL is empty, base URL is used for links.
acceptance_criteria: 1. mealie_public_url in config + setup wizard. 2. All template hrefs use public URL. 3. All API calls use base URL. 4. Empty public URL falls back to base URL silently.
notes: Mirrors the HA LAN-vs-Pangolin split already in project memory. mealie_base_url stays as http://192.168.1.170:3000 for API; mealie_public_url = https://mealie.korolev.link for links.
close_reason: mealie_public_url setting added; all browser links use it via settings.mealie_link_url(); API calls unchanged on base URL; setup wizard has both fields.

title: Split Mealie internal vs public URL
description: Mealie authenticates only on the LAN (http://192.168.1.170:3000) but the external URL is https://mealie.korolev.link/. Currently a single mealie_base_url is used for both API calls and browser links, so links in the UI point to the internal address. Need two settings: mealie_base_url (API/docker-internal, never opened by a browser) and mealie_public_url (used only for href generation in templates). API calls stay on the internal address; all 'Open in Mealie' links and recipe deep-links use the public URL. Setup wizard should expose both fields with a note that public URL can be left empty to fall back to base URL.
acceptance_criteria: 1. mealie_public_url added to config and setup wizard. 2. All template hrefs (Open Mealie buttons, recipe slug links) use public URL. 3. All httpx API calls use base URL. 4. Fallback: if public URL empty, base URL used for links too.
notes: Current single-URL approach works from the server but breaks browser links for users on the external network. The pattern mirrors the existing HA LAN-vs-Pangolin split already documented in project memories.
close_reason: Duplicate of 6eg.5 (done): mealie_public_url implemented. Grocy half not needed ΓÇö the app UI never links to Grocy; the Lovelace button URL is user-configured.

title: Stop echoing secrets into setup page HTML
description: setup.html renders API keys back as input values ΓÇö anyone with page access can read them. Mask saved values (show last 4 chars) and only overwrite when a new value is entered.
close_reason: Secrets never rendered into setup HTML; has-flags only; blank=keep, __CLEAR__=erase

title: Rotate all API keys + document rotation procedure
description: FoodAssistant API key, Grocy key, and any LLM keys pasted in shared contexts. Add a short docs section on where each key lives and how to rotate.

title: Backup/restore script for data volumes
description: Per decision in Phase 0: implement chosen backup for service/data, grocy/config, mealie/data + restore test.

title: Phase 1: Hardening & security
description: Key rotation, secrets handling, backups, image pinning.

title: Setup wizard: Grocy setup hints and link to documentation
description: The Grocy settings pane has no guidance for new users. Add: (1) a brief explanation of what Grocy is and why it is needed; (2) a link to the official Grocy documentation (https://grocy.info) and the FoodAssistant install guide; (3) a hint showing the default Grocy URL when running in pi_hosted mode (http://<hostname>.local:9383) and how to find the API key in Grocy (Settings > Manage API keys).

title: Setup wizard: warn user to save API key — not shown again (pi_hosted)
description: In pi_hosted mode the setup wizard generates an API key for the local stack but does not warn the user to copy it somewhere safe. After saving and navigating away the field is masked (write-only). Add a prominent one-time alert after the API key is generated: copy this key now, it will not be displayed again. Show it in a copyable <code> block with a clipboard button.

title: Satellite first sync: include ui_theme in SATELLITE_PULL_FIELDS
description: The first satellite config pull (and periodic pulls) do not sync ui_theme. A pi_remote device retains its own default theme instead of inheriting the server theme. Add ui_theme to SATELLITE_PULL_FIELDS in config.py so the satellite display matches the main server appearance. Device-local overrides should still be possible via the Interface pane.

title: features() flag: remove is_raspberry_pi() runtime import from hot path
description: settings.features() currently does 'from .hardware import is_raspberry_pi' at call time. This function reads /proc/device-tree which is slow and unavailable in CI. features() is called on every template render via theme_context(). Refactor: detect is_pi once at startup (store as a settings attribute or module-level constant), so features() is a pure dict lookup. This also makes features() safe to call in tests without mocking.

title: Recipes tab: browse, add, photo-import, and URL-import recipes
description: Add a Recipes page to the FoodAssistant UI so users never need to visit Mealie directly for day-to-day recipe work. Four capabilities in one tab: (1) Browse ΓÇö searchable list of Mealie recipes with name, description, and a link to open in Mealie; (2) URL import ΓÇö paste a webpage URL, backend fetches the page and sends it to the LLM to extract structured recipe data (name, description, ingredients, instructions, servings), then POSTs to Mealie API; (3) Photo import ΓÇö snap or upload a photo of a handwritten or printed recipe card, vision LLM extracts the recipe fields, user can review/edit before saving to Mealie; (4) Manual add ΓÇö simple form as fallback. All saves go to Mealie via its recipe create API (POST /api/recipes). The LLM extraction for photo and URL routes should reuse the existing VisionProvider / enrich_product pattern from providers/.
acceptance_criteria: 1. /ui/recipes page with nav link. 2. Recipe list loads from Mealie with search. 3. URL import: paste URL -> LLM extracts -> review form -> save to Mealie. 4. Photo import: upload/snap -> vision LLM extracts -> review form -> save to Mealie. 5. Manual add form. 6. Each saved recipe shows a confirmation with link to view in Mealie.
notes: URL import: fetch page HTML server-side (httpx), strip to readable text, send to LLM with a recipe-extraction prompt. Photo import reuses existing analyze pattern from routers/analyze.py. Mealie recipe create: POST /api/recipes with {name}, then PATCH /api/recipes/{slug} to add ingredients/instructions ΓÇö or use the scrape endpoint if Mealie supports it (POST /api/recipes/create/url).
close_reason: Recipes tab live: browse/search, URL import (Mealie scraper + LLM fallback), photo import via vision LLM, manual entry, shared review editor. extract_recipe on all 4 providers.

title: HA sensors + Lovelace card for meal plan & shopping
description: REST sensors: today/tomorrow dinner, shopping item count. Lovelace: meal plan card on the food dashboard. Remember: sensors use LAN URL, not Pangolin.

title: Add missing ingredients to shopping list (one click)
description: On each What-can-I-cook suggestion, button sends unmatched ingredients to the active Mealie shopping list. Backend: diff recipe ingredients vs inventory matches (already computed in suggest_recipes).
close_reason: Implemented ahead of Phase 0 sign-off (7cc.3 field-test, 7cc.5 approval) per Dan's direct request.

title: Cooked it: consume recipe ingredients from Grocy
description: Button on a suggestion/meal-plan entry that consumes matched inventory items in Grocy (reuse consume_stock + the ingredient matcher).

title: GitHub Actions: lint + tests on push/PR
description: ruff + pytest workflow; badge in README.

title: pytest suite for pure logic
description: Cover services/mealie.suggest_recipes + _tokens, services/barcode heuristics + LLM result handling, services/defaults.apply_defaults, providers/base.parse_json_response.

title: Route + template smoke tests
description: TestClient: every GET page returns 200, Jinja templates render, setup save round-trips. Mock Grocy/Mealie with httpx MockTransport.

title: Pin Docker image versions
description: grocy, mealie, ollama all use :latest ΓÇö pin to known-good tags and document upgrade procedure.

title: Phase 3: Mealie integration deepening
description: Close the loop between recipes, shopping list, and Grocy inventory.

title: Phase 2: Tests & CI
description: Automated test suite and GitHub Actions pipeline.

title: Docs pass: screenshots + HA guide refresh
description: README screenshots of inventory/mealplan/shopping; verify homeassistant/ docs match current sensor set.

title: Separate internal vs public URLs for Grocy/Mealie
description: API calls need the LAN/docker address; browser links want the public one (Pangolin). Add optional *_public_url settings used only for href generation.

title: PWA manifest + icons for phone use
description: Add manifest.json, icons, theme color so the UI installs to a home screen for quick scanning.

title: Barcode scan auto-checks shopping list item
description: When a scanned/committed pending item matches an unchecked Mealie shopping item (token match), check it off automatically.

title: Phase 4: Polish & UX
description: URL handling, mobile experience, docs.
